hero_subtitle: SFTY structures role-specific access and audit trails to help compliance teams, reviewers, and MLROs operate securely with clear governance and scalable operational control.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow

problem_title: Compliance control falters without clear permissions
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle when analyst, reviewer and MLRO roles lack precise access controls. This weakens governance, complicates audit trails and increases operational risk in regulated digital asset environments.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes compliance team roles by enforcing granular permissions, audit logs and segregation of duties to strengthen governance, maintain evidence integrity and support traceable audit trails for VASPs.
workflow_title: From role-based access to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures compliance team permissions, enabling controlled case workflow, actions, decision logging and evidence preservation for institutional audit readiness.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not just access control. Role-based evidence governance
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides a provider-neutral layer that enforces granular permissions while preserving audit-ready evidence and linking compliance decisions to secure governance workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides regulated VASPs with role-based access, evidence integrity and governance controls needed for secure deployment and audit-ready bank due diligence workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY help define role-based access controls
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how analyst and reviewer permissions support workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve evidence and decision history
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports governance for institutional compliance teams
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a centralized compliance control layer that connects provider outputs into structured workflows, decisions, cases and reports tailored for regulated digital asset compliance teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage compliance team permissions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports role-based access control, allowing fine-grained permission settings for analysts, reviewers, MLROs and operations teams to support appropriate workflow governance and segregation of duties.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and evidence packages with audit logs, supporting defensible review and traceability across compliance activities and internal or external audits.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, connecting their outputs without replacing any underlying compliance tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but integrates their outputs into one operational compliance workflow to reduce manual work and improve auditability.
final_title: Structure compliance team permissions with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY supports role-based access, audit logs and governance for controlled workflows and compliance operations.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
